steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The text opens by establishing that “several primates other than humans use tools” and then immediately uses an example: “For example, in Thailand, they have observed crab-eating macaques using stone tools.” The phrase “several primates” followed by a specific example signals that the macaques are one of multiple primate species that use tools.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A states that the macaques are “only one of several primate species to use tools”—this directly expresses the text's classification. Choices B, C, and D each claim something not mentioned in the passage: additional tools besides stone tools, skill comparisons between species, or a longer timeline of tool use. That matches choice A — A is correct.
